title: Erythranthe breviflora
taxonomy: Erythranthe breviflora is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships to other plant species. Erythranthe breviflora is further grouped into the genus Erythranthe, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to North America. The genus Erythranthe is part of the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that includes mint, snapdragons, and honeysuckle. Erythranthe breviflora is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Erythranthe guttata and Erythranthe grandiflora.
uses: Erythranthe breviflora is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Erythranthe breviflora is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. Further studies are necessary to fully understand its potential as a medicinal plant.

constituents: Erythranthe breviflora is known to contain a variety of active constituents, including iridoid glycosides such as eriodictyol, eriodictyol 7-rhamnoside, and leucosceptroside. These compounds have been found to possess potential bioactivities, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, the plant is also known to contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its overall bioactivity. intro: Erythranthe breviflora, commonly known as dwarf monkey flower or short-flowered monkey flower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the snapdragon family (Orobanchaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Erythranthe breviflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
